clifford.reason: Clifford algebra Cl(3) provides the rotor sandwich product R∘probe∘~R and norm ||·|| that define the fitness landscape; GA genome parameters are directly interpreted as Cl(3) multivector coefficients — the geometry is computed by clifford, not numpy

deap.reason: DEAP GA (blend crossover + Gaussian mutation + tournament selection) evolves the rotor genome across 20 generations; convergence below clifford distance 0.1 is the primary claim and is achieved entirely through DEAP's evolutionary loop

known_optimal_e1_to_e2.rationale: Known analytical rotor must achieve machine-epsilon clifford distance

known_optimal_e1_to_e3.rationale: Known analytical rotor must achieve machine-epsilon clifford distance

known_optimal_e2_to_e3.rationale: Known analytical rotor must achieve machine-epsilon clifford distance

identity_rotor_e1_vs_e2_far.rationale: Identity rotor leaves e1 unchanged; clifford distance from e2 equals ||e1-e2||=sqrt(2)~=1.414 (perpendicular basis vectors), must exceed 1.0

zero_genome_treated_as_identity.rationale: Degenerate zero-norm genome falls back to scalar identity; result equals ||e1-e2||=sqrt(2)~=1.414, must exceed 1.0 confirming fallback

random_genome_mean_distance_high.rationale: Random rotor parameters should not accidentally approach target; mean clifford distance over 50 random genomes must exceed 0.5

self_rotation_zero_distance.rationale: Identity rotor applied to self-target must yield exactly zero clifford distance

ga_beats_random_baseline.rationale: GA with 80 individuals and 25 generations on e1->e2 task must achieve clifford distance < 0.05, well below random baseline ~1.35

rotor_normalization_unit_constraint.rationale: Normalized rotor R must satisfy R*~R = 1 (unit rotor constraint in Cl(3))
